unit_label: Eukaryotes nested within Heimdallarchaeia as sister to Hodarchaeales
notes: Main-text synthesis: eukaryotes are reported as a well-nested clade within Asgard archaea and as sister to Hodarchaeales within Heimdallarchaeia after artifact-reducing treatments.

unit_label: Asgard ancestors show more duplications and fewer losses than other archaea
notes: Main-text synthesis: genome evolution in Asgard archaea involved significantly more gene duplication and fewer gene loss events compared with other archaea.
Main-text quantitative summary: the Hodarchaeales ancestor is reported with an estimated proteome size of 4,053 proteins, compared with 3,134 for LAsCA.

Article-level comparison: Hodarchaeales are reported as the largest Heimdallarchaeia subgroup by median genome size (5.1 Mb), compared with 3.5 Mb for Heimdallarchaeia overall and 2.4 Mb for Njordarchaeales.
Median computed from six manually transcribed Hodarchaeales OGT values stored in EME2023_HOD_OGT_VALUES_v1.csv; median=36.735 C, mean=37.728 C, min=27.09 C, max=48.67 C.
Main text reports that the Wood-Ljungdahl pathway was lost before the last common ancestor of Heimdallarchaeia and therefore before LAECA, implying a heterotrophic fermentative LAECA.

unit_label: EMP and partial oxidative PPP in Heimdallarchaeia/Hodarchaeales
notes: Main text reports that Heimdallarchaeia, including Hodarchaeales, retained an Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway and a partial oxidative pentose phosphate pathway, described as core modules of present-day eukaryotic central carbon metabolism.

unit_label: Hodarchaeales ancestor ETC complexes and nitrate reductase
notes: Main text reports that the last common ancestor of Hodarchaeales probably contained CI, CII, CIV and a nitrate reductase complex (NarGHIJ), indicating a membrane-associated respiratory support system before eukaryogenesis.

unit_label: epsilon DNA polymerase subunit only in Hodarchaeales
notes: Main-text ESP summary reports that the epsilon DNA polymerase subunit is only found in Hodarchaeales.

unit_label: L28e/Mak16 homologues specific to Njordarchaeales and Hodarchaeales
notes: Main-text ESP summary reports that ribosomal protein L28e (including Mak16) homologues are specific to Njordarchaeales and Hodarchaeales members.
